qa_pairs.question: What is Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD)?
qa_pairs.answer: Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD) is the domain of numerical analysis and physics concerned with the accurate and efficient simulation of fluid motions using computer software.

qa_pairs.question: What is OpenFOAM?
qa_pairs.answer: OpenFOAM is a suite of open source solvers and tools for Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD).

qa_pairs.question: Is OpenFOAM available on our clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, multiple versions of OpenFOAM are available as modules on our clusters.

qa_pairs.question: What kind of software is OpenFOAM?
qa_pairs.answer: OpenFOAM is an open source suite of solvers and tools for CFD.

qa_pairs.question: What is Fluent?
qa_pairs.answer: Fluent is a commercial Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD) program.

qa_pairs.question: Is Fluent available on our clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, Fluent is available on our clusters as part of the Ansys package.

qa_pairs.question: What is required to use Fluent on our clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: Users are required to supply a licence to use Fluent on our clusters.

qa_pairs.question: Which software package does Fluent belong to?
qa_pairs.answer: Fluent is part of the Ansys package.

qa_pairs.question: What is WRF?
qa_pairs.answer: WRF is a non-commercial program designed for atmospheric fluid dynamics.

qa_pairs.question: Is WRF available on our clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, WRF is available as a module on our clusters.

qa_pairs.question: What type of fluid dynamics does WRF focus on?
qa_pairs.answer: WRF is used for atmospheric fluid dynamics.

qa_pairs.question: Are Lattice Boltzmann techniques currently available?
qa_pairs.answer: No, Lattice Boltzmann techniques will be available later.

qa_pairs.question: Is Smoothed-particle hydrodynamics available at this time?
qa_pairs.answer: No, Smoothed-particle hydrodynamics will be available later.
